en: Our patient has a young man friend, to whom she is thinking of becoming engaged, she loves him and expects to be happy with him. At first nothing more is discoverable; but the investigator must not let himself be deterred by a negative result in the beginning of this preliminary questioning. When the direct way does not lead to the desired end, an indirect way may be taken. We accordingly turn our attention back to that strange moment when she ran away in front of the horses. We inquire who were her companions and what kind of social gathering was it, and find it was a farewell-party to her best friend, on her departure to a foreign health-resort on account of a nervous breakdown. We are told this friend is happily married and is the mother of one child. We may well doubt the assertion that she is happy. If she really were so, it is hardly to be supposed she would be "nervous" and in need of a cure. When I attacked the situation from a different vantage-ground, I learnt that our patient—after this episode—had been taken by her friends to the nearest safe place—her host's house. In her exhausted state he took charge of her. When the patient came to this part of her story, she suddenly broke off, was embarrassed, fidgeted and tried to turn the subject. Evidently some disagreeable reminiscences had suddenly cropped up. After obstinate resistances had been overcome, she admitted something very strange had happened that night. Her host had made her a passionate declaration of love, thus occasioning a situation that, in the absence of his wife, might well be considered both painful and difficult. Ostensibly this declaration came upon her like a "bolt from the blue." But a small dose of criticism applied to such an assertion soon apprises us that these things never do drop suddenly from the sky; they always have their previous history. It was a task of the following weeks to dig out piecemeal a long love-story. I will attempt to sketch in the picture as it appeared finally.
zh: 我們這位病人有一位年輕的男性友人，她正考慮與他訂婚；她愛他，並期望與他共度幸福時光。起初，並未發現更多線索；但研究者不可因初步詢問的負面結果而卻步。當直接途徑無法達到預期目標時，或許可採取間接方式。於是，我們將注意力轉回那個奇特的時刻——她在馬匹前逃跑的情景。我們詢問她的同伴是誰，以及那是何種社交聚會，結果發現那是一場為她摯友舉辦的告別派對，該友因神經衰弱而前往國外療養地。我們得知這位友人婚姻幸福，且育有一子。我們大可懷疑「她幸福」這項說法。若她真如此幸福，很難想像她會「神經衰弱」並需要療養。當我從另一個角度切入時，得知我們這位病人——在此事件之後——被朋友們帶到最近的避難所，即她主人的家中。在她筋疲力竭的狀態下，主人接手照顧她。當病人敘述到故事這一部分時，她突然中斷，顯得尷尬、坐立不安，並試圖轉移話題。顯然，某些不愉快的回憶突然湧現。在克服頑固的抗拒之後，她承認那晚發生了一件非常奇怪的事。她的主人向她熱烈表白愛意，從而造成了一個在他妻子缺席的情況下，可謂既痛苦又棘手的局面。表面上，這番表白對她而言猶如「晴天霹靂」。但只要對這種說法稍加批判，我們很快就會明白，這類事情絕不會憑空從天而降；它們總有其前因後果。在接下來的幾週裡，我們逐步挖掘出一段漫長的愛情故事。我將試圖勾勒出最終呈現的畫面。
